Notice Of Motion And Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-14-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Mikael Stahle, Esq. (310-531-1900; mstahle@maternlawgroup.com) of Matern Law Group, PC for Plaintiffs.
Dennis C. Huie, Esq. (415-956-2828; dhuie@rjo.com) of Rogers Joseph O'Donnell for Defendant.

Hearing is held (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFS ESTEFANIA PINEDA and CLAUDIA SALAZAR's Motion For Final Approval Of Class Action Settlement:

The motion for final approval is granted on the condition that the class representative service awards be reduced from $10,000 to $5,000 for each of the two named plaintiffs, with the difference ($10,000) to be included in the Net Settlement Payments to be distributed to class members. The Court finds this amount to be fair and reasonable in light of the amount of time (approximately 30 hours for each named plaintiff) and effort spent in connection with the litigation by the named plaintiffs and the average award to be received by participating class members, among other factors. (See, e.g., Fronda v. Staffmark Holdings, Inc. (N.D. Cal. June 1, 2018), 2018 WL 2463101, at *13-*15 [reducing requested service award for class representative from $10,000 to $5,000]; Munoz v. BCI Coca-Cola Bottling Co. of Los Angeles (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 399, 412 [$5,000 enhancement payments to two class representatives not an abuse of discretion].)

Counsel for Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Of Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-04-2021 Continued To Mar-04-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: Plaintiffs Estefania Pineda, Claudia Salazar's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Of Settlement.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' motion for preliminary approval of class action settlement is denied without prejudice, and the hearing is continued to March 4, 2021. By February 25, plaintiffs shall submit a further factual showing and a revised proposed settlement agreement sufficient to address and resolve the following issues:

1. Plaintiffs ask the Court to approve the class notice, which the moving papers indicate is attached to the proposed settlement agreement as Exhibit A. However, no proposed class notice was provided.
2. Paragraph 46 of the proposed settlement agreement provides that any charges and expenses of the settlement administrator greater than the estimated amount of $5,000 will be paid from the settlement amount. The Court will not approve that provision. The agreement must cap the settlement administrator's charges and expenses at a specific amount.
3. Paragraph 55 provides that defendant is not required to deposit the entire settlement amount until six months of the date of final approval. However, no explanation is provided for the delay, which reduces the present value of the overall proposed settlement.
4. The proposed settlement agreement is unclear as to the timing of distribution of settlement amounts. Paragraph 56 provides that the settlement administrator shall cause the payments to be mailed "on or before the fourteenth calendar day after the Effective Date or the thirtieth calendar day after the settlement is funded in accordance with Paragraph [55], whichever is later." Does that mean that if defendant splits the settlement amount into two payments as allowed, no distribution will be made until seven months after final approval? The proposed class notice, when it is drafted, must clearly disclose the anticipated timing of the settlement payments.
5. The Court cannot find that notice to class members by first-class mail only (Paragraphs 48 and 49) is the best notice practicable. Notice must be provided both by first-class mail and by email, if available.
6. The settlement agreement should also require the settlement administrator to establish a website where class members may access the pleadings, the proposed settlement agreement, and the class notice, as well as information regarding the fairness hearing.
7. The Court will not approve service awards to the two named class representatives without a detailed factual showing regarding their participation in the litigation. (See In re Cellphone Termination Fee Cases (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 1380, 1394-1395.)
8. The Court will not approve a final award of attorneys' fees without a factual showing by counsel sufficient to allow a lodestar cross-check. (See Laffitte v. Robert Half International, Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.5th 480, 505-506.)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)
